title: The Human Instrumentality Project In God We Trust and quot
display_title: The Human Instrumentality Project In God We Trust and quot
wikitext:
[]: The Human Instrumentality Project "In God We Trust&quot             Quote Post by Frank_langley » Sat Apr 14, 2012 5:03 pm HIP: The Human Instrumentality Project "In God We Trust" or In God We Trust is a film inspired in Neon Genesis Evangelion. Data: Is a Film of the Metal band alternative Figthstar and written, produced, directed and post-produced by the equipment of HITH. Figthstar has several songs with references to evangelion (SEELE-NERV or Shinji Ikari, Etc.) Official Trailer: [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m-TTwFujxU0[/url] Release Date: TBC "In God We Trust" imagine a near future in which society is on the brink of collapse due to the Earth's deteriorating environment. Multinational corporations and international governments have merged to form a small number of elite corporate power groups, each vying for ultimate power and control over the remaining populace. The story revolves around a momentous discovery made by high-ranking research scientist Piotr Drabik. Employed by a corporate entity known only as "ORTUS", Drabik believes that his research has enabled him to make direct contact with God. Chilling revelations are uncovered as Drabik discovers the true nature of God's will. As rumours spread, shady elements within ORTUS orchestrate Drabik's abduction in an attempt to subvert the end of days and realise their own nefarious ambitions. All the while, the highest echelons of the church are acutely aware of God's true will. Having kept their best defence against armageddon alive for centuries they prepare to fight once again. Caught in the middle of the web, Drabik must find the strength to confront his fears and ultimately decide the fate of us all. The only question which remains is whether humanity's future involves salvation or annihilation... _____________________________________________________________ ¡This is Amazing! XD

[]:             Quote Post by ONIAgent150 » Sat Apr 14, 2012 6:35 pm Well, apparently it's just a trailer to get studios interested. Those SEELE pillars... wow. Can we make that canon? The sheer imposing nature of them. Far scarier than the floating slabs.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Sat Apr 14, 2012 8:50 pm The fuck did I just watch? thing they have definitely down.

[]:             Quote Post by Kane of Nod » Sat Apr 14, 2012 8:59 pm This thread needs more slayer: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=K6_zsJ8KPP0

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Sat Apr 14, 2012 9:02 pm I was half expecting a gaint mecha to go running across the screen while watching that trailer.

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Sat Apr 14, 2012 9:17 pm This looks... interesting. I'm kind of feeling weird about watching it, though, since it's kind of weird about the Christian faith (to which I belong). "BUT WAIT HENRY, YOU LIKE EVANGELION!" Before anyone shouts that, I'd just like to say that I feel like the religious overtones in the series aren't particularly blasphemous. Plus, the Angels feel more like aliens than God-born creatures.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sat Apr 14, 2012 11:45 pm Too much COD footage and lawsuit-worthy Seele monolith rippage. 0/10 Would not bang

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Sun Apr 15, 2012 6:00 am So, when does the lawsuit come?

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sun Apr 15, 2012 10:21 am Not much out there about the film besides the trailer and what has already been mention in this thread. Wikipedia page: [url]http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human_Instrumentality_Project_%28film%29[/url] IMDB: [url]http://www.imdb.com/title/tt1247392/[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Sun Apr 15, 2012 10:24 am It interests me that IMDB lists the film as being released in 2010 . Is that related to the 40 minutes of footage that they've released?

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sun Apr 15, 2012 10:33 am Henry Spencer wrote: It interests me that IMDB lists the film as being released in 2010 . Is that related to the 40 minutes of footage that they've released?

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Apr 15, 2012 1:31 pm So what, they've just stolen a bunch of imagery, concepts and names from EVA and they expect no-one to notice or mind? Im not legal expert but surely you cant just half-inch a load of things like this and not face any consequences? Do the decent thing and send a link to Gainax/Khara/Anno or any other people you think should be aware of this. Its one thing to be influenced by other work (and lordy knows EVA got plenty of that), its quite another just to steal wholesale in this manner.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sun Apr 15, 2012 2:03 pm Henry Spencer wrote: Before anyone shouts that, I'd just like to say that I feel like the religious overtones in the series aren't particularly blasphemous. Plus, the Angels feel more like aliens than God-born creatures.

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Sun Apr 15, 2012 2:57 pm Dr. Nick wrote: It isn't even a matter of what you personally feel. The Angels in Eva ARE literally aliens.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Apr 15, 2012 3:01 pm Hmmm....judging by the sudden increase in quantity and ferocity of comments on the YT comments that word of this has got around the likes of 4chan. Im not inclined to get into flame wars about how this is nothing like what Eva is in terms of Ideon or the various western film influences. All I know is you just dont take a handful of things from some other work and spread them liberally around your own work. Or if you do, you ask first or at very very least add some clear acknowledgement. If they had said in the preamble under the video 'Yeah, we used some stuff from EVA, we're fans of the series and we thought it would look cool in areal world setting' then their wouldnt be a kerfuffle about it. @Jayfive: So including cross-shaped explosions, monoliths, and the image of Samael is enough to incite a lawsuit? Somehow, I don't think that argument would hold up in court.

[]:             Quote Post by Drabant » Sun Apr 15, 2012 3:12 pm Jayfive wrote: blah blah

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Apr 15, 2012 3:21 pm Drabant wrote: There isn't even a question where they got some of their inspiration from. Neon genesis evangelion is even in the tags for the video.

[]:             Quote Post by Drabant » Sun Apr 15, 2012 3:26 pm Jayfive wrote: herf

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Apr 15, 2012 3:33 pm Drabant wrote: But no, they shouldn't be surprised, but it would be a dick move to sue them over something as harmless as this.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Sun Apr 15, 2012 3:39 pm Depending on how open they are about this being a great big Eva reference this could be interesting. If there was ever a Western live-action Evangelion film, it would have to be somewhat distant to the anime itself. If you made this less generic rebel-looking soldiers shooting stuff and more of that shot of the crossplosion then this could be an interesting film. Unless they don't directly acknowledge Eva as their main inspiration. In which case fuck them.
